hypotheses.description: Life losses tend to occur when the ball y-position reaches zero, suggesting this state marks a miss or reset after failing to return the ball.
hypotheses.trigger: ball_y becomes 0 near a life decrement

hypotheses.description: Keeping paddle x close to ball x appears to increase the chance of surviving volleys and enabling later rewards.
Rewards occur when the ball is in active mid-screen trajectories, consistent with brick-hit opportunities during rally play.
hypotheses.trigger: ball in active non-reset flight with y above reset level

hypotheses.description: Using Fire outside clear serve/reset contexts correlates with no reward and multiple nearby life losses, so unnecessary Fire actions should be avoided.
hypotheses.trigger: fire action during active rally states

hypotheses.description: The core survival objective is to minimize transitions where lives decrease, as these cluster around failed return sequences.
hypotheses.trigger: pre-miss sequence before life counter drops
